calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($72,160 USD / 15,579 total beneficiaries) = $4.63 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 216 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.
Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

Our Work in Numbers for the Year: Special Surgeries - 118, Adoptions - 314, Rescues - 447, Animal Birth Control (ABC) Surgeries - 2427, Outpatients - 5172, Vaccinations - 7533
HUMANE ANIMAL SOCIETY (HAS) Annual Report 2023 - 2024 Our Work in Numbers for the Year

metrics.context_qualifier: Anti-Rabies and Multi-Component Vaccines administered to street animals and community pets during the reporting year.

source.quote: Vaccinations - 7533

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals treated as out-patients for various ailments at the facility during the reporting year.

source.quote: Outpatients - 5172

metrics.context_qualifier: Sterilization surgeries performed on street animals to control population during the reporting year.

source.quote: Animal Birth Control (ABC) Surgeries - 2427

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals rescued from danger and in need, brought into care during the reporting year.

source.quote: Rescues - 447

metrics.context_qualifier: Rescued dogs and pups successfully found loving homes during the reporting year.

source.quote: Adoptions - 314

metrics.context_qualifier: Specialized surgical procedures performed on animals during the reporting year.

source.quote: Special Surgeries - 118

The Humane Animal Society (HAS) has been honored with the World Rabies Day Award in the 'Animal Welfare Champion' category by the Global Alliance for Rabies Control (GARC) and Boehringer Ingelheim. This accolade recognizes HAS's 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control.

significant_events.summary: HAS was honored with the 'Animal Welfare Champion' award by GARC and Boehringer Ingelheim for 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control.

source.quote: The Animal Sensitization Certificate program, a collaboration between HAS and Bosch, successfully launched its second batch. This program empowers Bosch employees to aid street animals by teaching lifesaving and first aid skills.

significant_events.summary: Multiple batches of the program were conducted with Bosch employees, teaching first aid, rescue techniques, wound management, and puppy care.

source.quote: From September 22nd to 28th, we conducted a week-long Anti-Rabies Vaccination Drive in Coimbatore for World Rabies Day. Our goal was to vaccinate as many animals as possible and raise public awareness about the importance of vaccinating community pets. Despite the scorching sun, our dedicated team vaccinated 927 animals.

significant_events.summary: Multiple intensive Anti-Rabies Vaccination drives were conducted across city districts, including a week-long drive for World Rabies Day, vaccinating 927 animals.

significant_events.event_name: Legal Awareness Session for Coimbatore Police

source.quote: HAS has led the fight against animal cruelty in Coimbatore, facing challenges due to lack of legal awareness. To address this, we conducted a legal awareness session for rural police officers, supported by Coimbatore Superintendent of Police, Mr. V. Badrinarayanan (I.P.S). Dr. Mini Vasudevan explained the PCA Act and animal welfare laws. Educating law enforcement is crucial for prompt action against cruelty.

significant_events.summary: A legal awareness session was conducted for rural police officers in Coimbatore, explaining the PCA Act and animal welfare laws to fight cruelty.

source.quote: We conducted sessions on February 7th for primary and high school students of Yuvabharathi Public School. The interactive talks sparked active interest, and the school's fundraising activities raised substantial donations for our ABC and Rescue facility. Similarly, at Annai Velankanni Matriculation Higher Secondary School, we engaged around 550 students from grades 4 to 9 in discussions on animal welfare, inspiring thoughtful questions and compassion. We're grateful for these schools' efforts.

significant_events.summary: Sessions were conducted for primary and high school students on animal welfare, inspiring compassion and raising donations for the facility.

source.quote: On World Spay Day, HAS emphasized the importance of spaying and neutering community pets to reduce street animal populations. We performed 19 surgeries, helping 16 dogs and 3 cats secure a healthier future.

significant_events.summary: On World Spay Day, HAS emphasized the importance of spaying and neutering community pets, performing 19 surgeries (16 dogs, 3 cats).

source.quote: MARS, the company behind Pedigree and Royal Canin, partnered with our ABC and Rescue Center to promote indie dog adoptions. Their team provided essential training to our staff, focusing on adoption promotion and healthy diets for dogs.

significant_events.summary: Partnership with MARS (Pedigree and Royal Canin) to promote indie dog adoptions and provide essential staff training on adoption promotion and healthy diets.

source.quote: Pawga and the Humane Animal Society (HAS) united to host a joyful event where yoga enthusiasts and indie puppies came together. The gathering promoted yoga benefits and indie dog adoption, with playful pups spreading joy.

significant_events.summary: Multiple 'Pawga' events were held, blending yoga with rescue puppies to promote adoption and support welfare activities.

source.quote: We are thrilled to announce the successful completion of the Veterinary Assistant Training Program by Team HAS members at WVS India International Training Centre. Over three days, led by Ms. Elizabeth Peach from the US, the program covered anesthesia monitoring, sedation, drug administration, bandaging, and CPR. This opportunity expanded our knowledge and enhanced our veterinary care skills.

significant_events.summary: Team HAS members completed a three-day Veterinary Assistant Training Program covering anesthesia monitoring, sedation, drug administration, bandaging, and CPR.

source.quote: Our Founder and Managing Trustee, Dr. Mini Vasudevan, has been honored with the Krish Award by Sri Krishna Arts and Science College for her significant contributions to society and dedication to animal welfare.

significant_events.summary: Dr. Mini Vasudevan, Founder and Managing Trustee, was honored with the Krish Award for her significant contributions to society and animal welfare.

source.quote: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) received the Kerala Leadership Award for NGO Leadership on March 28th in Kochi, making it the only animal welfare organization among several recognized NGOs for their social contributions.

significant_events.summary: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) received the Kerala Leadership Award for NGO Leadership on March 28th in Kochi.

significant_events.event_name: Bosch CSR Project (Kennel renovation & kitchen revamp)

source.quote: We are delighted to report the successful progress of the Bosch CSR project at the HAS Animal Birth Control Centre. The kennel renovation and kitchen revamp, sponsored by Bosch, were celebrated with esteemed guests Naved Narayan, Centre Head - Coimbatore, BGSW, and Julius Panakkal, Section Head, BGSW.

significant_events.summary: The Bosch CSR project at the HAS Animal Birth Control Centre involved kennel renovation and kitchen revamp, promoting compassion through training and education.

source.quote: We are eternally grateful to Ford for sponsoring two sets of walking wheels for our paralyzed dogs affected by road accidents. Moved by their resilience, Ford's CSR team gifted the wheels to give these dogs the mobility they deserve.

significant_events.summary: Ford sponsored two sets of walking wheels for paralyzed dogs affected by road accidents, providing them with mobility.

source.quote: We recently enhanced our care with the help of Laura from Belgium, an expert in human physiotherapy. She shared valuable insights adapted for critically ill animals, resulting in rapid progress for our patients. This enlightening session, open to staff and volunteers, has equipped us with new skills we eagerly anticipate putting into practice.

significant_events.summary: An expert in human physiotherapy shared insights adapted for critically ill animals, enhancing staff and volunteer skills.

significant_events.event_name: A Workshop to Empower Community Members

source.quote: A recent training session at our ABC & Rescue Centre empowered individuals to assist animals in need more effectively. Participants, frustrated by their inability to help sick and injured animals, learned effective rescue techniques and shared their experiences.

significant_events.summary: A training session at the ABC & Rescue Centre empowered individuals to assist animals in need more effectively, teaching rescue techniques.

source.quote: Students from the Indian Public School, through Club Aura, raised funds for our four-legged friends by selling sustainable handmade items. They donated the proceeds to us, which we used to purchase vital items from our Amazon Wishlist.

significant_events.summary: Students from the Indian Public School raised funds for animals by selling handmade items, with proceeds used to purchase vital items.

significant_events.event_name: BeLove' campaign by Sri Krishna College of Technology

source.quote: Students of Sri Krishna College of Technology organized the 'BeLove' campaign, collecting essential items like rice and newspapers for our shelter. During their visit, they also provided physiotherapy to dogs in need, reflecting their genuine dedication.

significant_events.summary: Students collected essential items like rice and newspapers for the shelter and provided physiotherapy to dogs in need.

source.quote: Our dedicated volunteers embarked on a DIY mission to create a safe, dry haven for our puppies during rainy days. Their hard work and determination transformed the space into a comfortable sanctuary, showcasing their unwavering commitment and selfless efforts.

significant_events.summary: Dedicated volunteers created a safe, dry haven for puppies during rainy days, transforming the space into a comfortable sanctuary.

source.quote: In celebration of International Pet Day, we welcomed fifteen 8th graders from Chinmaya Vidyalaya CBSE School, Vadavalli, to our ABC and Rescue Centre. The students showed remarkable empathy for street animals and generously donated treats and food for our residents.

significant_events.summary: Fifteen 8th graders visited the ABC and Rescue Centre, showing empathy for street animals and donating treats and food.

source.quote: Students from Government College of Technology, Coimbatore, visited our shelter as part of their 'Pabulum' initiative, organizing a fundraiser and helping with various tasks, showing immense empathy and commitment to animal welfare. We also welcomed children from The Aalam school during their Circulus event to educate them about street animal hardships, fostering compassion and curiosity.

significant_events.summary: Students from Government College of Technology and The Aalam school visited the shelter, organizing a fundraiser and helping with tasks.

significant_events.event_name: Students from Sri Krishna College of Technology

source.quote: Seventeen students from Sri Krishna College of Technology visited our ABC and Rescue Center, engaging in various helpful tasks after their orientation. They maintained the premises, groomed the dogs, and generously donated rice and bed sheets. Their visit greatly supported our mission.

significant_events.summary: Students visited the ABC and Rescue Center multiple times, engaging in tasks like maintaining premises, grooming dogs, and donating.

significant_events.event_name: An Initiative by Rotaract Club of Coimbatore Spectrum

source.quote: Eleven members of the Rotaract Club of Coimbatore Spectrum showcased their love for animals by crafting comfortable platforms for our furry patients and helping feed them. Their compassion and efforts provided much-needed comfort, evident in our dogs' wagging tails.

significant_events.summary: Eleven members crafted comfortable platforms for furry patients and helped feed them, providing much-needed comfort.

source.quote: On August 26th, we celebrated International Dog Day at our ABC and Rescue Centre with dedicated volunteers. They walked, groomed, and showered our dogs with affection, inspiring many visitors to consider adopting a rescued dog.

significant_events.summary: Celebrated at the ABC and Rescue Centre with dedicated volunteers who walked, groomed, and showered dogs, inspiring adoptions.

source.quote: Our latest session, 'Art with Puppies,' saw enthusiastic participation, with proceeds supporting our animal welfare initiatives. This event not only provides a pleasant weekend but also raises awareness about the challenges faced by our furry friends and the impact of our collective efforts.

significant_events.summary: A session saw enthusiastic participation, with proceeds supporting animal welfare initiatives and raising awareness.

significant_events.event_name: Support from Inner Wheel Club of Coimbatore

source.quote: We gratefully received a compassionate donation from the Inner Wheel Club of Coimbatore, accepted by our trustee, Dr. Madhu Ganesh. This support aids our efforts in caring for animals in need.

significant_events.summary: Received a compassionate donation from the Inner Wheel Club of Coimbatore, accepted by trustee Dr. Madhu Ganesh.

source.quote: Welcome the new year with our EveryLife 2024 calendar, featuring captivating rescue dogs and cats and their inspiring stories. These calendars make thoughtful gifts, with all proceeds supporting HAS animals. By this month all the copies were almost sold out.

significant_events.summary: Launched the EveryLife 2024 calendar featuring rescue dogs and cats, with all proceeds supporting HAS animals.

significant_events.event_name: Fundraiser Event from Ladies Circle of Coimbatore

source.quote: The Ladies Circle of Coimbatore organized a fundraiser involving their children to foster empathy for animals in need. The youngsters generously donated their savings and visited our ABC and Rescue Center to see the impact of their contributions.

significant_events.summary: Children organized a fundraiser, donating savings and visiting the ABC and Rescue Center to foster empathy for animals.

source.quote: Embracing the Christmas spirit, we organized 'Click for a Cause,' where people captured moments with our furry friends against a Christmas wish tree. Participants brightened the day for our residents.

significant_events.summary: A Christmas event where people captured moments with furry friends against a Christmas wish tree, brightening the day for residents.

source.quote: In celebration of World Forestry Day on March 22nd, and as part of the Green Tamil Nadu Mission, we organized a tree planting event at our ABC and Rescue Centre.

significant_events.summary: A tree planting event was organized at the ABC and Rescue Centre as part of the Green Tamil Nadu Mission.

source.quote: Our Work in Numbers for the Year: Special Surgeries - 118, Adoptions - 314, Rescues - 447, Animal Birth Control (ABC) Surgeries - 2427, Outpatients - 5172, Vaccinations - 7533
HAS has led the fight against animal cruelty in Coimbatore, facing challenges due to lack of legal awareness.

data.original_text: 20 million pets are living in poverty, of which 70 percent have never seen a veterinarian.
Over half of owners living below the poverty line have been unable to pay for something their pets needs.
We're a global leader in animal welfare, working to end animal suffering and promote compassionate living.
Humane World for Animals works to end animal cruelty and suffering in all its forms and fights to create lasting change.
Our sanctuaries: Creating safe, nurturing environments where rescued animals receive care and a peaceful place to call home.

We fight the big fights to end suffering for all animals. Together with millions of our supporters, the Humane Society of the United States takes on puppy mills, factory farms, the fur trade, trophy hunting, animal cosmetics testing and other cruel industries.
Through our rescue, response and sanctuary work, as well as other direct services, we help thousands of animals in need every year.

Humane Society International. We work around the globe to promote the human-animal bond, rescue and protect dogs and cats, improve farm animal welfare, protect wildlife, promote animal-free testing and research, respond to disasters and confront cruelty to animals in all its forms.
American Humane Society has championed the human-animal bond, rescuing, protecting and advocating for animals in need.

As rescuers, we deploy resources, technology and personnel with urgency and compassion to rescue animals from danger and disaster. As advocates, we are the voice of the voiceless, shaping and driving policy to establish and expand protection for all animals. As trailblazers, we develop and implement programs that build meaningful connections among servicemembers, change-makers and communities working with animals — fostering growth, healing and understanding for all.
We rescue, transport, and rehabilitate thousands of animals every year. Our sanctuaries provide safe, lifelong homes for animals with nowhere else to go.
Humane World for Animals trains first responders and communities to protect animals during disasters—so families never have to leave their pets behind.
The Arizona Humane Society (AHS) is a safety net for the most vulnerable animals—the sick, injured and abused—who are often turned away by other shelters.
We never euthanize a pet for space or length of time.

We help pets who are at risk for euthanasia at other shelters by transferring them into our care. We accept those that are easily adoptable as pets, as well as those that require special care due to experiences of abuse or neglect.
In addition to our Center for Pets, we also operate a regional spay/neuter clinic, which performs approximately 8,000 spay/neuter surgeries annually.
In 2021, with your help, we alleviated suffering for millions of farm animals and helped make the business of food production, service and providers more humane.
We won victories in Utah and Nevada, banning the confinement of egg-laying hens and mandating cage-free conditions. In Nevada, we also helped pass a ban on the sale of eggs from caged hens.

With our support, Washington and Illinois and 30 more localities enacted measures against pet store sales of puppy mill puppies. The new laws mean that 35 pet stores, including 12 Petlands, must stop selling puppies. Our success in 2021 means we have now helped five states and more than 400 municipalities break the puppy mill-pet store pipeline by banning retail puppy sales.

Our investigators uncovered a link between law enforcement suspects in Kentucky with dogfighting rings in Australia and helped a local animal protection group there secure a warrant that led to the rescue of 27 dogs.
Nevada Humane Society: 140k+ total animals saved. 8,501 adoptions in 2025. 31,016 volunteer hours in 2025. 4,051 active donors in 2025.

IN INDONESIA, where HSI is a founding member of the Dog Meat Free Indonesia Coalition, bans on dog meat were enacted in 18 cities and regencies, including provincial capitals of Surabaya (East Java) and Semarang (Central Java). There are now 21 dog and cat meat-free jurisdictions in Indonesia, saving thousands of animals from these brutal trades every month.
Medan became North Sumatra province's first city to ban the dog meat trade. This was a huge victory—up to 200 dogs were being slaughtered in Medan every day.
OUR ANIMAL RESCUE TEAM, with the support of our shelter and rescue partners, removed nearly 4,000 beagles during the summer of 2022 from a mass breeding facility.
Lynchburg Humane Society: Last year, our Lynchburg Center for Pets helped 13,034 pets and 13,233 people.
American Humane Society: 1+ billion Farm animals protected under our welfare standards. 85,000+ Animals fed, sheltered and rescued. 500,000 Animals protected at zoos and aquariums.
